auto_fails: decorative_mascot: Data holds one identical standing pose the whole runtime, just perched on China's bar tip (hook@0.3 → seg1:end) then parked on the waffle fill edge (seg2:start → payoff@-0.4) — position tracks the data but there is no setup→action→payoff bit.

junk_imagery.evidence: No photos or AI cutouts anywhere — every frame is native vector chart/waffle art (hook@0.3, seg2:mid).

decorative_mascot.evidence: Data holds one identical standing pose the whole runtime, just perched on China's bar tip (hook@0.3 → seg1:end) then parked on the waffle fill edge (seg2:start → payoff@-0.4) — position tracks the data but there is no setup→action→payoff bit.

bare_number_card.evidence: Numbers always sit inside a demonstration — values ride inside the bars at seg1:end (1425.9 / 1417.2 / 338.3) and percentages annotate the filling waffle at seg3:mid.

dead_air.evidence: longest_static_s=0.0 and bars/waffle cells are advancing in every sampled frame (hook@0.8 → hook@2.2 shows continuous bar growth); max_dup_run 8 ≈ 0.33s.

empty_void.evidence: dark_fraction=0.0; the card is populated with five labelled bar tracks plus legend even at the emptiest moment (hook@0.3), though the lower band there is unused.

one_line: This is a competent chart export with a mascot sticker riding the bar — the hook is the single worst thing: 2.8 seconds of an empty five-bar template slowly filling while the caption sits on top of the chart.
problems: Hook fails outright: at hook@0.3 the frame is a titled but EMPTY bar template ('most populous countries' + five zero-length tracks) — the rubric explicitly forbids opening on a slow chart build; nothing has happened by hook@0.8.

Caption text is mispositioned over the artwork for the entire first beat — 'Two countries' at hook@0.3 and 'hold more' at hook@0.8/seg0:start crash straight through Data and China's bar, while the dedicated caption band at the bottom of the frame sits empty; from seg1:start onward the caption correctly moves down to that band, so beat 1 is a layout bug.
Data is decoration with a coordinate: identical standing pose from hook@0.3 all the way to payoff@-0.4. He never gestures, reacts, or does a bit — he is a marker glued to the fill front.

Legend color integrity is broken at seg2:start–payoff@-0.4: the 'United States' swatch is the same purple as 'Rest of world', and in the bar beat China renders purple while its legend swatch is pink — a viewer cannot map color to country.
The callout bubble at seg3:start–payoff@-0.4 permanently occludes the 'Rest of world' legend row, and the source credit is clipped off the left edge in every frame ('ource: United Nations…').
Only two visual ideas across 28s — one bar race (0–11s) and one waffle fill (14–28s) — with the bar chart holding the same composition for roughly eleven seconds.
Effective cadence is 12.8fps with a 0.465 duplicate ratio: the motion that exists will read as judder, not as smooth animation.

Payoff is a text bubble, not a visual landing — 'Eight countries, over half the planet' arrives at seg3:start as a paragraph while the waffle keeps filling underneath, so the punchline and the demonstration don't hit together.
fixes: Replace the first 2s: open on the waffle grid already slamming shut — 36 of 100 cells snapping to China+India color in one hard beat with Data bracing against the impact — and let the ranked bar chart come second as the breakdown.
Move the beat-1 captions into the lower caption band used from seg1:start onward, and never let caption text render over the chart card or the mascot.

Give Data one real bit per beat: have him try to hold back the China bar and get shoved to the right edge by it in the bar beat, then physically stack/kick the last few waffle tiles into place on the 35% line at seg2:end.
Fix the legend palette so each series has a unique color matching its rendered geometry (China pink everywhere or purple everywhere; give the US its own hue distinct from 'Rest of world').
Reposition the closing callout so it doesn't cover the legend — dock it to the empty lower band and shrink it to one line ('8 countries. Half the planet.').
Raise the render/interpolation rate so unique-frame cadence approaches the export fps; the 0.465 duplicate ratio must come down before this passes on smoothness.
Split the 11s bar beat into two distinct staged visuals (top-2 vs the field, then the US dropping in at a quarter the height) so no composition holds longer than ~4s.
Land the payoff visually: hold the final 36-vs-190 waffle split with the rest-of-world cells scattering into 190 tiny fragments on 'Wild, right?', with Data reacting to it.
